title: Phase 1A: wire ferray-fft into ferrotorch-core::fft (close 10-fn fft gap)
description:
Add ferray-fft as a workspace dep and wrap its API in ferrotorch-core::fft. ferray-fft has the exact 10 functions ferrotorch is missing per docs/audit/01-ferrotorch-core.md: fftn, ifftn, rfftn, irfftn, hfft, ihfft, hfftn, ihfftn, fftfreq, rfftfreq, fftshift, ifftshift.

Scope:
- Add ferray-fft = workspace dep in ferrotorch-core/Cargo.toml.
- Wrap each ferray-fft fn as a Tensor<T> -> Tensor<T> wrapper in ferrotorch-core/src/fft.rs, mirroring the existing fft/ifft/fft2/ifft2/rfft/irfft pattern (Tensor -> ferray::Array round-trip).
- Add re-exports to ferrotorch-core/src/lib.rs.
- Add tests round-tripping fft -> ifft for each new fn.
- Add corresponding differentiable wrappers in grad_fns/fft.rs for fftn/rfftn/irfftn (the autograd-relevant subset).

GPU discipline (CRITICAL):
- ferrotorch-gpu currently has NO cuFFT bindings. ferrotorch-core's existing fft/fft2/ifft/ifft2/rfft/irfft are CPU-only.
- DO NOT silently route GPU tensors through host. If input.device() != Cpu, return FerrotorchError::DeviceUnavailable with a message naming cuFFT, NOT a t.to(Cpu) -> compute -> t.to(device) sandwich. Document this explicitly in module-level docs.
- The GpuBackend trait in gpu_dispatch.rs would need new fft methods if/when GPU fft lands; that's a separate issue.
- Apply /rust-gpu-discipline pre-flight checklist before claiming the task done: search for forbidden patterns (.to(Cpu) inside compute fns, silent device fallbacks).

Closes ~38% of the audit-flagged fft gap (current 6 fns -> 16/16 torch.fft fns).
